description:
Bring vex-chat in line with the four sibling repos (types-js, crypto-js, libvex-js, spire) for hygiene, security posture, and release tooling. Audit findings after installing @vex-chat/libvex@2.0.0 and comparing all five repos surfaced 13 gaps, split across three buckets: (a) pure hygiene / supply-chain hardening (10 items), (b) a release-story decision for the publishable packages, and (c) per-app release workflows for Tauri desktop and Expo mobile backed by 2026 best-practice research.

See child issues. Landing order: hygiene PR first (items 1-8), then the release-story decision (item 9), then the two app release workflows in parallel once the decision is made.

## Context
- 4 siblings on npm + changesets/action; vex-chat on pnpm 10.30.3 catalog with no release pipeline
- libvex 2.0.0 just released; vex-chat catalog bumped from 1.2.0-rc.15 to 2.0.0
- pnpm 10 already blocked lifecycle scripts for libvex/types/crypto on install — the deny-by-default behavior the research flagged as a material improvement over npm --ignore-scripts
- vex-chat uses mise.toml not .tool-versions; monorepo has path-filtered CI via dorny/paths-filter; packages/store and packages/ui both 'private: true' so no changesets needed unless that changes
- Research reports covered: Tauri v2 GitHub release flow, Expo/RN release patterns, pnpm CI/CD best practices — findings embedded in each child issue
title: vex-chat sibling-repo baseline sync

description:
Every other sibling repo (types-js, crypto-js, libvex-js, spire) has a .tool-versions file with 'node 24.14.0' (and pnpm in vex-chat's case). vex-chat uses mise.toml instead.

Both can coexist — mise reads either — but actions/setup-node@v6.3.0's node-version-file: input natively reads .tool-versions without needing the jdx/mise-action step. Adding it keeps CI simple and matches the sibling baseline.

## Change
- Create .tool-versions at repo root containing 'node 24.14.0' and 'pnpm 10.30.3'
- Leave mise.toml alone — local dev flow stays identical
- Update any CI workflows that reference mise to use setup-node with node-version-file: .tool-versions instead (see research findings in parent epic)
title: Add .tool-versions alongside mise.toml

close_reason: Superseded — single-branch main (no pre-mode). See replacement issue.
description:
One-time setup for the develop/master two-branch flow with changesets pre-release mode.

## Prereq
vex-chat-6gm.7 (release story decision) must be resolved. If the answer was 'private-forever no changesets', skip this issue entirely. If Option 2 'changesets for publishable packages', scope this to the publishable set. If Option 3 'changesets for app versioning only', this issue is in scope.

## 1. Install changesets
```
pnpm add -D -w @changesets/cli @changesets/changelog-github
pnpm changeset init
```

## 2. Write .changeset/config.json
```json
{
  "$schema": "https://unpkg.com/@changesets/config@3/schema.json",
  "changelog": [
    "@changesets/changelog-github",
    { "repo": "vex-protocol/vex-chat" }
  ],
  "commit": false,
  "fixed": [],
  "linked": [],
  "access": "restricted",
  "baseBranch": "develop",
  "updateInternalDependencies": "patch",
  "ignore": [],
  "privatePackages": { "version": true, "tag": true }
}
```

Key lines:
- baseBranch: develop (not master — develop is the integration point)
- No linked/fixed: apps bump independently
- updateInternalDependencies: patch cascades store/ui changes to both apps automatically
- privatePackages.tag: true creates git tags for private apps so GitHub Releases can hang off them

## 3. Enter pre mode once on develop
```
git checkout develop
pnpm changeset pre enter rc
git add .changeset/pre.json
git commit -m 'chore: enter rc pre-release mode'
git push
```

## 4. Add AGENTS.md release section
Document:
- develop/master topology (what branch does what)
- libvex catalog bump convention: when pnpm-workspace.yaml bumps @vex-chat/libvex, the changeset MUST name both @vex-chat/mobile and @vex-chat/desktop manually (changesets can't see catalog deps via the workspace graph)
- The release PR ceremony (pre exit + version + rm pre.json + commit + merge + re-enter on develop)
- Override escape hatch: if the accumulated RC bumps underclaimed, add an extra changeset in the release branch before running pre exit
- Link to changesets docs: https://github.com/changesets/changesets/blob/main/docs/prereleases.md

## 5. Set up branch protection
- master: require PR from develop, require CI green, require PR-time 'Guard master' check (see vex-chat-6gm.12)
- develop: require PR, require CI green, require 'Guard develop' checks (changeset present + pre.json in mode pre)

## Acceptance
- Config file committed to develop
- pre.json committed to develop with mode: pre
- AGENTS.md has the release section
- Branch protection rules set in GitHub UI
- One dry-run feature PR to develop passes the 'Guard develop' check

## Blocked by
vex-chat-6gm.7 (release story decision)
title: Changesets pre-release mode on develop + config.json + AGENTS.md ceremony docs

close_reason: Superseded — auto-changeset target branch changes from develop to main, prompt needs regeneration. See replacement issue.
description:
Port the auto-changeset workflow from libvex/types/crypto/spire to vex-chat with a monorepo-specific prompt.

## Shape
Identical skeleton to libvex's .github/workflows/auto-changeset.yml:
- Triggered on pull_request [opened, synchronize]
- Concurrency group: auto-changeset-<PR#>, cancel-in-progress: true
- Guarded: user.type != Bot, head.repo == repository, title doesn't start with 'chore: version packages' OR 'chore: release v'
- Uses pnpm/action-setup@v6 + setup-node@v6 with cache: pnpm
- anthropics/claude-code-action@v1

## What changes from libvex
No tarball download step. libvex compares PR-built dist against the published npm tarball to verify user-visible impact byte-for-byte. vex-chat apps are private, nothing to compare against — source diff is enough. Skip 'Build PR' + 'Download published tarball' steps.

## Prompt decision tree
1. Read AGENTS.md + top of CHANGELOG.md for voice
2. Read git log of origin/${base_ref}..HEAD
3. git diff --name-only origin/${base_ref}...HEAD

4. Map changed files to affected workspaces:
   apps/mobile/**            → @vex-chat/mobile
   apps/desktop/**           → @vex-chat/desktop
   packages/store/**         → @vex-chat/store  (cascades to both apps)
   packages/ui/**            → @vex-chat/ui     (cascades to both apps)
   packages/eslint-config/** → @vex-chat/eslint-config (no cascade, dev-only)
   pnpm-workspace.yaml       → inspect the diff:
       libvex catalog bump → manually list BOTH @vex-chat/mobile + @vex-chat/desktop
       other catalog bump → usually empty changeset
   .github/**, .tool-versions, tsconfig*, eslint.config.js,
   prettier configs, *.md                   → empty changeset (dev-only)

5. Bump-level decision per affected workspace:
   major — removed public exports, changed signatures, breaking UI/config removals.
           For apps: change requires users to migrate state, re-login, re-enter creds.
   minor — new exports, new screens/flows, new settings, backward-compat additions.
   patch — bug fixes, internal refactors, dep bumps not changing surface.
   Bias toward patch; when in doubt between patch and empty, prefer empty.

6. Write .changeset/<slug>.md:
   - Non-empty: YAML frontmatter listing affected packages + bump types, followed by
     1-3 sentences describing user-visible impact in the CHANGELOG voice.
   - Empty: exactly '---\n---\n' when no user-visible change.

7. git add .changeset/ + commit 'chore: add changeset' + push origin HEAD:${head_ref}

## Title guards
In addition to standard 'chore: version packages' skip, also skip 'chore: release v*' — the promotion PR already contains the bump locally and must not get a stacking Claude changeset.

## Interaction with pre mode
Zero. Pre mode lives in .changeset/pre.json; the auto-changeset workflow never touches it. Claude-written changesets get consumed by 'changeset version' in pre mode the same as any human-written changeset.

## Acceptance
- .github/workflows/auto-changeset.yml committed
- Dry-run PR opened with a single file change in packages/store: Claude writes a @vex-chat/store patch changeset that cascades to both apps on version
- Dry-run PR opened with only a .github/** change: Claude writes an empty changeset
- Dry-run PR that bumps libvex in pnpm-workspace.yaml: Claude writes a changeset naming BOTH @vex-chat/mobile and @vex-chat/desktop

## Blocked by
vex-chat-6gm.10 (changesets setup must exist first)
title: Auto-changeset workflow (Claude) adapted for vex-chat monorepo

close_reason: Superseded — release flow replaced with two-APK dev/release workflows + app.config.js conditional. See replacement issue.
description:
Three workflow files plus two guard workflows implementing the dev-RC/master-stable release flow.

## Files to create

### .github/workflows/release-develop.yml
Trigger: push to develop
Runs changesets/action with version: 'pnpm changeset version' and publish: 'pnpm run release:tag-only' (a local script that creates and pushes mobile-v/desktop-v tags for the private apps since 'changeset publish' skips private packages).
Calls reusable _build-mobile.yml + _build-desktop.yml downstream, gated on whether each app's version field actually changed in the merged commit (outputs.publishedPackages from changesets/action).
Profile: development-rc. OTA update published to EAS branch 'development'. GitHub Release marked --prerelease.
Concurrency group: release-develop, cancel-in-progress: false.

### .github/workflows/release-master.yml
Trigger: push to master
Three jobs:
1. guard — fails loudly if .changeset/pre.json exists (belt-and-suspenders on top of the PR-time guard in pr-check-master.yml)
2. detect-versions — checks if apps/mobile/package.json or apps/desktop/package.json version field changed in this push (compares HEAD vs HEAD~1)
3. build-{mobile,desktop}-stable — conditional on detect-versions outputs, calls reusable build workflows with profile: production. OTA NOT published (skip step). GitHub Release marked --latest.

### .github/workflows/_build-mobile.yml (reusable via workflow_call)
Inputs: profile (development-rc | production), prerelease (bool)
Steps: checkout, pnpm, setup-node, expo CLI, 'eas build --platform android --profile ${profile} --local --output build.apk', conditional 'eas update --branch development' ONLY if profile == development-rc, gh release create/upload with --prerelease or --latest flag.
iOS flow: macos-latest runner, 'eas build --platform ios --profile ${profile} --local', 'eas submit --platform ios' (production profile only).

### .github/workflows/_build-desktop.yml (reusable via workflow_call)
Inputs: profile, prerelease
Steps: matrix macos-latest ARM + macos-latest Intel + ubuntu-22.04 + windows-latest, tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0 with projectPath: apps/desktop, tagName: ${github.ref_name}, releaseDraft: true, includeUpdaterJson: true. Secrets: TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY, APPLE_* for notarization, Azure Trusted Signing for Windows (covered in vex-at4.19 follow-ups).

### .github/workflows/pr-check-master.yml
Trigger: pull_request, branches: [master]
Single job that fails if .changeset/pre.json exists. Checks mode is not 'pre'. Reviewers see red check at PR review time, branch protection blocks the merge.

### .github/workflows/pr-check-develop.yml
Trigger: pull_request, branches: [develop]
Two guards:
1. Changeset required: 'pnpm changeset status --since=origin/develop' must succeed (Claude will have written one; human PRs must too)
2. pre.json must exist with mode: pre (catches the 'forgot to re-enter pre mode' bug — develop is SUPPOSED to be in pre mode continuously)

## eas.json profiles needed
```json
{
  "build": {
    "development-rc": {
      "channel": "development",
      "distribution": "internal",
      "android": { "buildType": "apk" }
    },
    "production": {
      "channel": "production",
      "distribution": "store",
      "android": { "buildType": "app-bundle" }
    }
  }
}
```

Both profiles have 'updates.enabled: true' in app.json. What makes production 'OTA-off' is that release-master.yml never runs 'eas update' — the channel is wired but no updates flow.

runtimeVersion.policy: 'fingerprint' in app.json so the same commit produces the same fingerprint under both profiles (OTAs are compatible at the fingerprint level; we just choose not to send them to production).

## Tag format
mobile-v1.2.0-rc.3 (develop, --prerelease)
mobile-v1.2.0      (master, --latest)
desktop-v1.2.0-rc.3 / desktop-v1.2.0
Always pass --prerelease for RC tags so GitHub UI collapses them under a Pre-release section and doesn't displace the Latest badge.

## Secrets needed
- EXPO_TOKEN
- GOOGLE_SERVICES_TOKEN (Android submit)
- APPLE_* trio for iOS submit + notarization (see vex-at4.19)
- TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY + password
- Eventually: Azure Trusted Signing for Windows (see vex-at4.19 follow-up)

## Acceptance
- All five workflow files committed
- Dry-run release-develop.yml merges a feature PR and produces a mobile-v*-rc* tag + GitHub pre-release with APK
- Dry-run release PR (pre exit + version) opens; pr-check-master.yml passes
- A PR that forgot to delete pre.json gets blocked by pr-check-master.yml
- A feature PR with no changeset gets blocked by pr-check-develop.yml

## Blocked by
vex-chat-6gm.10 (changesets setup)
vex-chat-6gm.11 (auto-changeset workflow to supply the changesets)
title: Release workflows: develop RC + master stable + PR-time guards

description:
Set up changesets in regular (non-pre) mode on `main` for the vex-chat monorepo. Replaces the long-lived develop + pre-mode plan from vex-chat-6gm.10 after research showed Bluesky (the only production RN project with automated OTA from a long-running branch) collapsed to a single-branch + fingerprint-gate model instead.

## Rationale for the pivot
Research findings (see session notes from the pivot conversation):
- Expo's official versioning model: `package.json.version` bumps once per cycle (human PR), not per OTA. `versionCode` / `buildNumber` is the monotonic per-binary identifier, managed by EAS with `appVersionSource: "remote"`. `runtimeVersion` (fingerprint policy) is the OTA compatibility key.
- Bluesky's `package.json` is currently `1.121.0` while their latest GitHub Release is `1.120.0` — one PR titled "Bump version" happens per cycle, then every merge ships as an OTA under that version.
- Expensify's `9.3.58-7-staging` counter is the closest production analogue to what changesets pre-mode gives you, but they ship native on every counter bump, not OTAs.
- No production RN project runs two long-lived branches with pre-mode OTA routing.

## Install
```bash
pnpm add -D -w @changesets/cli @changesets/changelog-github
pnpm changeset init
```

## `.changeset/config.json`
```json
{
  "$schema": "https://unpkg.com/@changesets/config@3/schema.json",
  "changelog": [
    "@changesets/changelog-github",
    { "repo": "vex-protocol/vex-chat" }
  ],
  "commit": false,
  "fixed": [],
  "linked": [],
  "access": "restricted",
  "baseBranch": "main",
  "updateInternalDependencies": "patch",
  "ignore": [],
  "privatePackages": { "version": true, "tag": true }
}
```

Key decisions:
- `baseBranch: "main"` — single long-lived branch
- No `pre.json`, no pre-mode anywhere
- Apps bump independently via `updateInternalDependencies: "patch"` — cascades `packages/store` and `packages/ui` changes to both apps automatically
- `@changesets/changelog-github` auto-links PRs + author handles in generated CHANGELOG.md
- `privatePackages: { version: true, tag: true }` — private apps get version bumps AND git tags (required for GitHub Releases to hang off something)

## AGENTS.md release section

Document the release cycle:
1. Features merge to `main`. Claude writes a changeset per PR (see sibling issue 14).
2. `changesets/action` keeps a rolling "Version Packages" PR current against `main`.
3. When ready to cut a release: merge the "Version Packages" PR — this bumps `package.json` version(s) and writes CHANGELOG entries.
4. Go to Actions → "Release APK" → Run workflow with the new version as input.
5. GitHub Release lands with the production APK attached and marked `--latest`.

Also document the **libvex catalog bump convention**: when `pnpm-workspace.yaml` bumps `@vex-chat/libvex`, the changeset must explicitly name BOTH `@vex-chat/mobile` and `@vex-chat/desktop` because changesets can't see catalog deps via the workspace graph. Claude's auto-changeset workflow handles this automatically but document the rule so humans writing changesets manually follow the same pattern.

## Branch protection
- `main`: require PR + CI green (lint, typecheck, test jobs, `pr-check-main`)
- No other long-lived branches

## Acceptance
- `.changeset/config.json` committed to main
- `.changeset/README.md` committed (from `changeset init`)
- `AGENTS.md` has the release section + libvex catalog bump convention
- No `.changeset/pre.json` anywhere (regular mode, not pre mode)
- Branch protection rules set in GitHub UI

## Blocked by
vex-chat-6gm.7 (release story decision — if Option 1 "private forever", this issue is skipped; if Option 2 publish packages or the app-versioning-only path, this is in scope)
title: Changesets regular mode on main + config.json + AGENTS.md ceremony docs

description:
Port the auto-changeset workflow from libvex/types/crypto/spire to vex-chat. Same skeleton as the four sibling repos already have, adapted for the pnpm monorepo shape and the single-branch regular-changesets-mode plan.

## Differences from libvex's version
- **No `Build PR` step, no `Download published tarball` step** — vex-chat apps are private, nothing to compare against. Source diff is enough.
- **pnpm/action-setup@v6 + setup-node with `cache: pnpm`** instead of plain setup-node
- **Target base is always `main`** (no develop branch)
- **Skip rules**: `user.type != Bot` AND title doesn't start with `chore: version packages` (the changesets/action PR) AND same-repo only

## Workflow skeleton
```yaml
name: Auto Changeset
on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize]

concurrency:
  group: auto-changeset-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  generate-changeset:
    if: |
      github.event.pull_request.user.type != 'Bot' &&
      !startsWith(github.event.pull_request.title, 'chore: version packages') &&
      github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name == github.repository
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
    steps:
      - uses: step-security/harden-runner@v2.17.0
        with: { egress-policy: audit }
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          ref: ${{ github.head_ref }}
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v6
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with: { node-version-file: .tool-versions, cache: pnpm }
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts

      - name: Configure git
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Check existing changeset
        id: check
        run: |
          if pnpm changeset status --since=origin/main 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "exists=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "exists=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

      - name: Generate changeset with Claude
        if: steps.check.outputs.exists == 'false'
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          prompt: |
            [decision tree below, expanded in six-step libvex format]
          claude_args: |
            --allowedTools "Bash(git diff:*),Bash(git log:*),Bash(git add:*),Bash(git commit:*),Bash(git push:*),Bash(ls:*),Bash(cat:*),Read,Write,Grep,Glob"
```

## Claude decision tree
1. Read `AGENTS.md` and the top entries of `CHANGELOG.md` for voice
2. `git log origin/main..HEAD --format="%s%n%b%n---"`
3. `git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD`
4. Map changed paths to workspaces:
   - `apps/mobile/**` → `@vex-chat/mobile`
   - `apps/desktop/**` → `@vex-chat/desktop`
   - `packages/store/**` → `@vex-chat/store` (cascades to both apps via `updateInternalDependencies`)
   - `packages/ui/**` → `@vex-chat/ui` (cascades to both apps)
   - `packages/eslint-config/**` → `@vex-chat/eslint-config` (no cascade, dev-only)
   - `pnpm-workspace.yaml`:
     - libvex catalog bump → explicitly list BOTH `@vex-chat/mobile` and `@vex-chat/desktop`
     - other catalog bumps → usually empty changeset (catalog is mostly dev tooling)
   - `.github/**`, `.tool-versions`, `tsconfig*`, `eslint.config.js`, prettier configs, `*.md` → empty changeset (dev-only)
5. Pick a bump per affected workspace:
   - **major** — removed public exports, changed signatures, breaking UI/config removals. For apps: change requires users to migrate state, re-login, re-enter credentials.
   - **minor** — new exports, new screens/flows, new settings, backward-compat additions.
   - **patch** — bug fixes, internal refactors, dep bumps that don't change surface.
   - Bias toward patch. Empty when no user-visible change. When in doubt between patch and empty, prefer empty.
6. Write `.changeset/<slug>.md`:
   - Non-empty: YAML frontmatter listing affected packages + bump types, followed by 1-3 sentences describing user-visible impact in the CHANGELOG voice.
   - Empty: exactly `---\n---\n` (no frontmatter, no body).
7. `git add .changeset/` + `git commit -m "chore: add changeset"` + `git push origin HEAD:${{ github.head_ref }}`

## Acceptance
- `.github/workflows/auto-changeset.yml` committed
- Dry-run PR with a single `packages/store` change: Claude writes a `@vex-chat/store: patch` changeset. When `changeset version` runs, cascades to both apps as patches.
- Dry-run PR with only a `.github/**` change: Claude writes an empty changeset
- Dry-run PR that bumps libvex catalog in `pnpm-workspace.yaml`: Claude writes a changeset naming BOTH `@vex-chat/mobile` and `@vex-chat/desktop`

## Blocked by
vex-chat-6gm.13 (changesets regular mode setup must exist first)
title: Claude auto-changeset workflow for vex-chat monorepo (main branch, no pre-mode)

description:
**Hard requirement**: two distinct APKs, one with Expo OTA enabled at the native config level and one with it hard-disabled. Users pick which install experience they want — continuous dev updates (OTA-backed) or pinned release binaries (no dynamic update surface, audit-friendly).

## Triggers at a glance

| APK | Trigger | Cadence |
|---|---|---|
| **Release APK** (OTA disabled) | Push to `main` that changes `apps/mobile/package.json` version field | Once per release cycle, when changesets "Version Packages" PR is merged |
| **Dev APK** (OTA enabled) | Push to `main` + fingerprint change detected | Rare — only when native surface (deps, config plugins, native code) changes |
| **OTA update to dev channel** | Push to `main` + fingerprint unchanged | Every JS-only merge |

## `apps/mobile/app.config.js` — profile-conditional `expo.updates`

```js
export default ({ config }) => {
  const devMode = process.env.EAS_BUILD_PROFILE === 'development';
  return {
    ...config,
    expo: {
      ...config.expo,
      name: devMode ? 'Vex Dev' : 'Vex',
      android: {
        ...config.expo?.android,
        package: devMode ? 'chat.vex.mobile.dev' : 'chat.vex.mobile',
      },
      updates: devMode
        ? {
            enabled: true,
            url: 'https://u.expo.dev/<YOUR_PROJECT_ID>',
            fallbackToCacheTimeout: 0,
            checkAutomatically: 'ON_LOAD',
          }
        : { enabled: false },
      runtimeVersion: devMode
        ? { policy: 'fingerprint' }
        : { policy: 'appVersion' },
    },
  };
};
```

Key design notes:
- **Different `android.package`** (`.dev` suffix) so both APKs can coexist on one device. Without different package names, installing one overwrites the other.
- **Different `name`** so launcher icons are visually distinct.
- **`updates.enabled: false`** hard-disables the expo-updates runtime in the release APK. No network calls, no OTA code path active.
- **`runtimeVersion` policies differ**: fingerprint for dev (deterministic OTA compatibility), `appVersion` for release (value never consulted because updates are disabled).

## `apps/mobile/eas.json`

```json
{
  "cli": {
    "version": ">= 12.0.0",
    "appVersionSource": "remote"
  },
  "build": {
    "development": {
      "channel": "development",
      "distribution": "internal",
      "android": { "buildType": "apk" },
      "autoIncrement": true
    },
    "production": {
      "distribution": "internal",
      "android": { "buildType": "apk" },
      "autoIncrement": true
    }
  }
}
```

Note: `production` has NO `channel` field. No EAS Update wiring at all, belt-and-braces on top of `updates.enabled: false`.

## Workflow 1 — `.github/workflows/dev-apk.yml`

**Trigger**: `push: main`, `workflow_dispatch`
**Concurrency**: `dev-apk`, `cancel-in-progress: true`

**Logic**:
1. Check fingerprint via `npx @expo/fingerprint .` against the hash stored in the rolling `mobile-dev-latest` release body (previous dev build's fingerprint)
2. **Unchanged** → `eas update --branch development --message "main <sha>"` (OTA push only, ~30 seconds)
3. **Changed** → `eas build --profile development --platform android --local --output dev.apk` → emit two GitHub prereleases:
   - **Durable per-build**: `mobile-dev-v<version>-<sha>` with APK attached, `--prerelease` flag, body includes fingerprint hash + package name
   - **Rolling pointer**: `mobile-dev-latest` — delete + recreate each time with `--cleanup-tag` so the tag always points at the current commit. Same APK attached. Body explains the rolling semantics.

**Distribution for testers**:
- Bookmark `https://github.com/vex-protocol/vex-chat/releases/tag/mobile-dev-latest` — one URL forever
- Or `gh release download mobile-dev-latest --pattern '*.apk' -R vex-protocol/vex-chat`
- Install once → receive OTAs on every subsequent JS-only merge to main
- Reinstall only when fingerprint changes (rare; ~weekly at most)

**Per-build tags**: surface a specific historical dev build for rollback. Rare to need, permanent to keep.

## Workflow 2 — `.github/workflows/release-apk.yml`

**Trigger**: `push: main` with `paths: ["apps/mobile/package.json"]`. No manual dispatch.
**Concurrency**: `release-apk`, `cancel-in-progress: false`

**Logic**:
1. **Detect step**: compare `apps/mobile/package.json` version at HEAD vs HEAD~1 (`git show HEAD~1:apps/mobile/package.json`). Guards against whitespace-only edits to the file.
2. **Build step (conditional on version change)**:
   - `eas build --profile production --platform android --local --output release.apk`
   - `gh release create mobile-v<version> release.apk --latest --title "Mobile <version>" --generate-notes`

**Zero OTA steps**. Never calls `eas update`. The release APK has `updates.enabled: false` baked in and the workflow doesn't even wire up a channel.

**Automation flow**:
1. Features merge to main. Claude writes changesets.
2. `changesets/action` keeps a "Version Packages" PR current.
3. You merge the Version Packages PR.
4. That merge changes `apps/mobile/package.json` version field → `release-apk.yml` fires automatically → builds production APK → GitHub Release lands.

No human-clicked "Run workflow" step. The release IS the Version Packages PR merge.

## Workflow 3 — `.github/workflows/pr-check-main.yml`

**Trigger**: `pull_request: [main]`

**Single job**: fail if `.changeset/pre.json` exists. Sanity check; should never happen in regular-mode changesets but cheap to verify.

## Secrets required
- `EXPO_TOKEN` — EAS CLI authentication
- `GITHUB_TOKEN` — automatic, for `gh release create`

## Explicitly NOT needed
- Apple Developer Program ($99/yr) — no iOS builds
- Google Play developer account ($25 one-time) — no AAB output, no Play submission
- Azure Trusted Signing — covered under vex-at4.19 (Tauri desktop, separate track, currently deferred)
- macOS runners — all builds run on `ubuntu-latest`

## Summary: where each artifact lives

| Artifact | Tag | Prerelease flag | Rebuild trigger | Who installs |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Release APK | `mobile-v1.2.0` | `--latest` | Auto on version bump merge to main | End users |
| Dev APK (rolling pointer) | `mobile-dev-latest` | `--prerelease` | Auto on fingerprint change on main | Testers, first install only |
| Dev APK (per-build history) | `mobile-dev-v1.2.0-a1b2c3d` | `--prerelease` | Auto on fingerprint change on main | Rollback reference |
| OTA update | (no tag, no release) | — | Auto on every JS-only merge to main | Existing dev APK installs |

## Acceptance
- `app.config.js`, `eas.json`, and all three workflow files committed
- Dry-run: merge a JS-only PR to main → `dev-apk.yml` fires, publishes OTA to `development` channel, no new APK build
- Dry-run: merge a PR that adds a native config (e.g. a new Expo plugin) → `dev-apk.yml` fires, fingerprint changed, builds new dev APK, creates BOTH the durable `mobile-dev-v<version>-<sha>` prerelease AND updates the rolling `mobile-dev-latest` prerelease (same APK attached to both)
- Dry-run: merge the changesets Version Packages PR on main → `release-apk.yml` fires automatically, detects the version change, builds production APK, creates `mobile-v<version>` release with `--latest` flag, NO OTA pushed anywhere
- **Manual test**: install both APKs on a physical device. Verify two icons (`Vex Dev` + `Vex`). Verify dev APK receives OTAs when triggered. Verify release APK does not contact any update server (use `adb logcat | grep -i update` to confirm silence).
- **Rollback test**: grab an earlier `mobile-dev-v<version>-<sha>` from the history, install it, verify it still receives OTAs (its fingerprint matches its own era, OTAs targeting that fingerprint keep arriving until the fingerprint boundary is crossed)

## Blocked by
- vex-chat-6gm.13 (changesets regular mode — release workflow depends on version bumping from changesets)
- vex-chat-6gm.14 (auto-changeset — so Claude writes changesets that drive the version bumps)
title: Two-APK release workflows: dev APK with OTA, release APK with updates.enabled=false

description:
vex-chat has a .prettierrc but no .prettierignore. The four sibling repos all have one.

## Change
Add .prettierignore at repo root with (minimum):
- node_modules
- dist/
- pnpm-lock.yaml
- apps/desktop/src-tauri/target/
- apps/mobile/android/build/ + apps/mobile/ios/build/
- packages/ui/output/
- .beads/

Rationale: prevents accidentally reformatting generated/vendored files or Rust/native Android build output, which is what the sibling baseline does for the equivalent dirs.
title: Add .prettierignore

description:
ci.yml currently uses a shallow checkout and runs lint/audit unconditionally across every workspace. Two related fixes:

## 1. fetch-depth: 0 on every actions/checkout
Required for 'pnpm --filter "...[origin/master]"' to see the merge base. Without it the selector silently returns zero packages and all the git-aware filters become no-ops.

## 2. Wire pnpm --filter "...[origin/master]"
Replace unconditional 'pnpm lint' / 'pnpm typecheck' / 'pnpm test' with 'pnpm --filter "...[origin/master]" <cmd>'. The '...' prefix includes dependents, so a change in packages/store will still trigger apps/desktop + apps/mobile checks transitively.

Native pnpm filtering is the canonical 2026 pattern — no Turborepo / Nx required at this workspace size (6 projects).

## References
- https://pnpm.io/filtering
- https://www.warpbuild.com/blog/github-actions-monorepo-guide
- byCedric/expo-monorepo-example for Expo+pnpm monorepo CI patterns
title: Add fetch-depth: 0 + pnpm workspace filtering to ci.yml

description:
Root package.json has dev/lint/format/license:check but no test/typecheck/build entry points. Per-app scripts exist but there's no single monorepo-wide invocation.

## Change
Add to root package.json:
```json
"scripts": {
  "build": "pnpm -r --if-present build",
  "test": "pnpm -r --if-present test",
  "typecheck": "pnpm -r --if-present typecheck"
}
```

The '--if-present' flag means packages without the script are skipped silently, so this works during the period when packages/store has no tests yet (see the separate tests task).
title: Add root scripts: test/typecheck/build fan-out

description:
Three pnpm-native hardening steps that the sibling-repo research (QuiiBz/sherif + pnpm 10 supply-chain docs) flagged as concrete wins.

## 1. Sherif (catalog/version drift linter)
Add to lint job: `pnpm dlx sherif@latest`. Rust-based, ~28ms, zero-config. Catches version mismatches across workspace packages that escaped catalog-ification. Complements catalogs by failing CI when someone adds a dep at a different version than what catalog already defines.
https://github.com/QuiiBz/sherif

## 2. catalogMode: strict
In pnpm-workspace.yaml:
```yaml
catalogMode: strict
```
Fails 'pnpm install' if someone adds a dep with a different version than catalog's entry. Only turn this on after running 'pnpm dlx sherif' once to clean up any existing drift.

## 3. minimumReleaseAge + blockExoticSubdeps
In pnpm-workspace.yaml:
```yaml
minimumReleaseAge: 604800   # 1 week
blockExoticSubdeps: true
```
minimumReleaseAge blocks freshly-published versions from landing — mitigates Sha1-Hulud-style fast supply-chain attacks. blockExoticSubdeps rejects transitive git/tarball deps.

## Context
pnpm 10 already blocks lifecycle scripts by default (caught libvex/types/crypto during the recent install — see bvefr7hpi task log). These three add more layers on top of that default.

## References
- https://pnpm.io/supply-chain-security
- https://socket.dev/blog/pnpm-10-0-0-blocks-lifecycle-scripts-by-default
- GHSA-379q-355j-w6rj (git-dep script bypass — vex-chat unaffected, all workspace deps are workspace:*)
title: Add Sherif + pnpm supply-chain guardrails

description:
## Symptom
Fresh 'pnpm install' fails with ERR_PNPM_META_FETCH_FAIL for @vex-chat/* packages unless Verdaccio is running at localhost:4873. The committed .npmrc contains:

```
@vex-chat:registry=http://localhost:4873
//localhost:4873/:_authToken=local-dev-token
```

Hit during the libvex 2.0.0 install on 2026-04-10 — had to override with 'npm_config_@vex-chat:registry=https://registry.npmjs.org/' to unblock.

## Options

### Option A — remove .npmrc scope override, rely on ENV override when needed
Move the Verdaccio scope override out of the committed .npmrc. Devs who want to test against Verdaccio export 'npm_config_@vex-chat:registry=http://localhost:4873' in their shell or use a local .npmrc.local that is gitignored.

### Option B — committed .npmrc points at public npm, scripts/publish-local.sh flips it
Keep .npmrc public by default. The publish-local.sh / install-local.sh helper scripts temporarily rewrite .npmrc for the local-verdaccio flow and restore it at the end.

### Option C — use --registry flags in dev scripts
Keep the committed .npmrc pointing at Verdaccio. Add an 'install:public' script that runs 'pnpm install' with --config.registries.@vex-chat=https://registry.npmjs.org/ so fresh clones have a one-liner escape hatch.

Option A is the cleanest for new contributors. Option B is best for people who actively use Verdaccio daily. Pick one and document in README.

## Acceptance
Fresh clone + `pnpm install` (no Verdaccio running) installs successfully.
title: Fix .npmrc Verdaccio trap for fresh clones

description:
vex-chat currently has:
- Root package.json: "name": "vex-chat", "version": "1.2.0-rc.7", "private": "true"
- packages/store: "private": true, "version": "0.1.0"
- packages/ui: "private": true, "version": "0.0.0" (Mitosis compiler output, not a publishable npm package)
- packages/eslint-config: "private": true (local only)
- apps/desktop, apps/mobile: both private

The root version field (1.2.0-rc.7) implies someone expected a release story, but every workspace member is private. Nothing will ever publish via changesets as-is.

## Decision to make
Either:

### Option 1: private-forever
Accept that vex-chat will never publish to npm. Everything stays private. Actions:
- Remove 'version' from root package.json (it's meaningless on a private package)
- Consolidate all internal deps via workspace:* (they already are)
- Skip changesets entirely
- The Tauri and Expo app versions (see sibling issues) are the only ones that matter, tracked in their respective config files (Cargo.toml, app.json)

### Option 2: publish packages/store and/or packages/eslint-config
Flip 'private: false' on the packages worth sharing. Wire up changesets + release.yml following the libvex pattern but with 'pnpm publish -r --access public --no-git-checks --provenance'. packages/store makes sense because external consumers might want the vex SDK's state layer; packages/eslint-config makes sense as @vex-chat/eslint-config for external projects.

## Reasoning notes
- The research confirmed 'pnpm publish -r' rewrites workspace:* specifiers to real versions at publish time, so the monorepo shape is compatible
- changesets/action works with pnpm — no special config beyond publish: 'pnpm ci:publish' and --no-git-checks
- changesets handles independent versioning per-package within one release PR, so Option 2 doesn't force lockstep versioning
- One big release PR is the right shape until ~15+ packages (research finding)

## Blocks
This decision gates all other 'add release pipeline' work for packages/*. If Option 1, skip changesets setup entirely. If Option 2, need release.yml + auto-changeset.yml added.

## Acceptance
Decision recorded in this issue with a short writeup of the chosen option and why. Follow-up issues for the mechanical work get created as discovered-from links.
title: Decision: release story for packages/* — private forever or publishable via changesets

description:
packages/store has zero tests. This is the state layer for a crypto chat app — it owns identity, session state, message atoms, server/channel CRUD. It's exactly the part of the codebase that benefits most from unit coverage.

## Why critical
The audit found every sibling repo has tests:
- types-js: tsc --noEmit + type-level assertions
- crypto-js: vitest (covers primitives + encoding round-trips)
- libvex-js: vitest unit + node/browser e2e against spire
- spire: vitest

packages/store has the least coverage AND the highest blast-radius on bugs (losing an identity atom or corrupting a session record = user data loss). That's the wrong direction.

## Scope for a first pass
Not 'full coverage' — just the state invariants and service-layer contracts:

1. **Identity atom** — create/load/persist/rotate the per-device identity key
2. **Session atom** — X3DH session ratchet state, sealed before write, unsealed on read
3. **Server CRUD** — createServer() -> channels.retrieve() read-after-write (service.ts:200-211)
4. **Channel CRUD** — createChannel() -> channels.retrieve() read-after-write (service.ts:178-191)
5. **Invite flow** — joinInvite() -> servers.retrieveByID() -> channels.retrieve() (service.ts:254-271)

Mock the libvex Client surface in the first pass. Once vex-chat-lok (MSW mocks from OpenAPI/AsyncAPI) lands, migrate these to use the real mocked wire instead of hand-rolled stubs.

## Acceptance
- vitest.config.ts added to packages/store
- 'pnpm --filter @vex-chat/store test' runs green
- CI picks up the new 'test' script via the root fan-out (see sibling hygiene task)
- At least the 5 flows above have at least one test each
title: Add vitest unit tests to packages/store

close_reason: Superseded by new two-APK plan with hard-disabled OTA on release binary. See replacement issue filed this session.
description:
Add GitHub Actions workflows for apps/mobile following the Bluesky 3-file pattern. Research confirmed this is the 2026 best-practice reference for an open-source Expo + RN release pipeline.

## Reference implementation
https://github.com/bluesky-social/social-app/.github/workflows/
- build-submit-android.yml — eas build --local on ubuntu, separate AAB for Play + APK for side-load, eas submit
- build-submit-ios.yml — macos-26-xlarge, Xcode 26.4, eas build --local, eas submit, dSYM upload
- bundle-deploy-eas-update.yml — fingerprint-gated OTA that auto-degrades to a native rebuild

## Tasks

### eas.json
Three existing profiles (development, preview, production) plus a new **production-apk** profile:
```json
"production-apk": {
  "extends": "production",
  "android": { "buildType": "apk" }
}
```
Uses a SEPARATE dedicated side-load keystore (NOT the Play upload key). Once Play and side-load signatures diverge the two installs can never coexist on a device — plan the split up front.

Also set:
```json
"cli": { "appVersionSource": "remote" },
"build": { "production": { "autoIncrement": true } }
```
EAS auto-increments versionCode/CFBundleVersion server-side.

### app.json
Set `runtimeVersion.policy: "fingerprint"` — the hash covers native surface area (deps, config plugins, native code). OTAs can't ship to incompatible binaries by construction.

### GitHub Releases integration
Add a step that downloads the production-apk build artifact from EAS and runs 'gh release upload' to attach it to the GitHub Release. This is the side-load path for privacy-conscious users — first-class distribution for a crypto chat.

### iOS
App Store + TestFlight only via 'eas submit'. No IPA on GitHub Releases. First submission manual to seed App Store Connect metadata.

### Notifee
Confirm config plugin is pinned and working with the release build image. Remote push needs FCM service account JSON (Android) + APNs .p8 (iOS) via 'eas credentials'.

## Cost
- Apple Developer Program: $99/yr (mandatory for iOS distribution at all — no loophole)
- Google Play: $25 one-time
- EAS Build free tier: 15 Android + 15 iOS builds/month — enough for solo cadence
- EAS Update free tier: 1,000 MAU — migrate to self-hosted Xavia OTA when crossing the threshold

## Related
- vex-at4.19 covers Tauri desktop — this is the mobile counterpart
- References deep research report on React Native release best practices (2026-04-10)
title: Expo mobile release workflow — EAS Build + side-load APK + fingerprint OTA

description: Bootstrap fails because (1) the mise android-sdk plugin needs yq on PATH to parse its metadata, and (2) the postinstall hook requires mise experimental=true. Make bootstrap reproducible by declaring yq as a project mise tool and installing it before the full mise install.
notes: Approach: revert mise.toml changes; preflight check in bootstrap.sh requires a real yq on PATH (not a mise shim — shim dispatches via mise and fork-bombs by recursing into android-sdk list-all). Cleaned up broken yq shim at ~/.local/share/mise/shims/yq.
title: Fix bootstrap.sh: declare yq in mise.toml and enable experimental hooks

description: In initSocket(), on 'open' event send JSON.stringify({ type: 'auth', token: this.token }) as first message. Remove the headers: { Cookie } option from WebSocket constructor. Wait for server to accept auth before proceeding. Same code path for all platforms (Node, browser, Tauri, RN).
title: [libvex] Send auth message on WS open

description: Add JWT_SECRET env var for JWT signing. Stop reusing the NaCl server signing key (SPK) for HMAC-SHA256 JWT signatures. Generate with: node -e "console.log(require('crypto').randomBytes(64).toString('hex'))". Fall back to SPK if JWT_SECRET not set (backward compat). Auth-comparison doc flagged this as a key hygiene concern.
title: [spire] Separate JWT_SECRET from SPK

description: Each Client creates its own axios.create() instance instead of using the global ax import. After login/loginWithDeviceKey, set Authorization: Bearer on the per-client instance. Delete addCookie(), getCookies(), this.cookies[], and the isNode check. Remove browser-or-node remnants.
title: [ADR-008] libvex: per-client axios + Bearer headers

description: Add setTimeout(0) yield between initSocket() and negotiateOTK() in connect(). OTK generation creates 100 Ed25519 key pairs synchronously (~5s on mobile), blocking the event loop and preventing the WS open callback from firing. Without the yield, the auth message never sends before the server timeout.
title: [libvex] Event loop yield before OTK generation

description: On wss.on('connection'), wait for first message: { type: 'auth', token: JWT }. 10-second timeout — no auth = close. Valid JWT → create ClientManager, proceed with challenge flow. Invalid → send { type: 'unauthorized' }, close. Replaces the current cookie-based express-ws middleware auth.
title: [spire] Post-connection WS auth handler

description: Device-key challenge-response issues a 1-hour JWT instead of 7-day. The device key can re-auth cheaply, so long-lived tokens are unnecessary exposure. Password login keeps 7-day expiry since re-entering password is expensive for the user.
title: [spire] 1-hour JWT for device-key auth sessions

description: Remove express-ws dependency. Create WebSocketServer({ noServer: true }). Handle HTTP upgrade via server.on('upgrade') → wss.handleUpgrade(). Change initApp() return type from expressWs.Application to express.Application. Remove express-ws and @types/express-ws from deps.
title: [spire] Replace express-ws with raw ws + noServer

description: Integration test: two users create a server, create a channel, both join via invite, both send messages to the channel, both receive each other's messages. Tests group key distribution and channel messaging end-to-end.
title: [test] Group messaging in channels

description: Validate that after Phase 2, closing and reopening the desktop (Tauri) and mobile (RN) apps auto-connects without re-entering password. Device key from OS keychain signs challenge → JWT issued → connect succeeds. Test with fresh install, restart, and keychain-cleared scenarios.
title: [test] Auto-login works on Tauri and RN after restart

description: Two new routes: POST /auth/device accepts { deviceID, signKey }, looks up device, returns { challenge: hex(nonce) }. POST /auth/device/verify accepts { deviceID, challenge, signed }, verifies Ed25519 signature against stored signKey, issues 1-hour JWT + cookie. Challenge stored in-memory with TTL, single-use, same pattern as action tokens.
title: [spire] Device-key challenge endpoints

description: Install eslint-plugin-jsdoc in the shared ESLint config. Enforce require-jsdoc with publicOnly:true on all public methods, classes, and functions in libvex, types-js, crypto-js. Add require-param and require-returns as warnings. TSDoc syntax. This ensures bots, AI agents, and app developers get autocomplete with descriptions.
title: ESLint jsdoc plugin — enforce public API documentation across all repos

description: Integration test: two Client instances (clientA, clientB) with different users. clientA sends DM to clientB, clientB receives and decrypts it. Proves the full X3DH key exchange works between different users, not just self-DM. Requires per-client axios (ADR-008) to avoid cookie conflicts.
title: [test] Two-user direct messaging

description:
Ship `@vex-chat/types/mocks` subpath export that produces MSW v2 handlers from openapi.json and asyncapi.json, enabling SDK consumers (bots, CLI, app dev loops, vitest tests) to run against a realistic in-process mock without Docker.

## Why

Complements Microcks (vex-at4.15 / vex-at4.16 — CI contract testing, public demos) with an in-process option for the hot path: SDK unit/integration tests, Tauri desktop dev loop, Expo mobile dev loop. Zero-Docker dev loop, sub-millisecond intercept, browser/Node/RN parity.

## Engine choice (from research)

- HTTP: @mswjs/source (fromOpenApi) — runtime, official mswjs org, uses spec examples first then seeds from schema. No codegen, no drift. ~9.9k weekly downloads, maintained by kettanaito.
- WebSocket: hand-rolled fromAsyncApi() — no tool exists. Built on @asyncapi/parser (already in types-js devDeps) + MSW 2 ws.link() API (stable since Nov 2024). Estimated 150-300 lines.
- Type-safe overrides (optional secondary export): openapi-msw wrapper.
- Stateful layer: @mswjs/data.

Rejected: msw-auto-mock (build-time codegen, two sources of truth), orval (overkill for mocks alone).

## Statefulness — REQUIRED

Audit of packages/store/src/service.ts, apps/desktop, apps/mobile confirms 3 explicit read-after-write flows:

1. createServer() -> channels.retrieve(serverID) (service.ts:200-211)
2. createChannel() -> channels.retrieve(serverID) (service.ts:178-191)
3. joinInvite() -> servers.retrieveByID() -> channels.retrieve() (service.ts:254-271)

Plus atom-driven read-backs in CreateServerModal.svelte and ChannelBar.svelte that depend on the service updating $servers/$channels atoms BEFORE returning. Mobile AddServerScreen.tsx has the same pattern.

Stateless mocks are insufficient. Use @mswjs/data to model server/channel/user/invite resources as an in-memory DB. ~300-400 LOC of state management.

Flows that do NOT need statefulness:
- Optimistic local updates: delete server, setAvatar, markRead — tests assert local atom changes
- WS-push delivery: sendDM, sendGroupMessage — tests inject frames via link.broadcast()

## Package layout

  types-js/src/mocks/
    index.ts        createVexMocks(), setupVexMockServer()
    openapi.ts      fromOpenApi wrapper with @mswjs/data bridge
    asyncapi.ts     hand-rolled AsyncAPI -> ws.link() generator
    db.ts           @mswjs/data models (server/channel/user/invite)
    typed.ts        (optional) openapi-msw typed overrides

## Consumer API

  import { setupVexMockServer } from '@vex-chat/types/mocks'
  const server = await setupVexMockServer({ seed: 42 })
  beforeAll(() => server.listen({ onUnhandledRequest: 'error' }))
  afterEach(() => server.resetHandlers())
  afterAll(() => server.close())

## Package.json changes

- Add msw, @mswjs/source, @mswjs/data as OPTIONAL peer deps (consumers opt in)
- Add ./mocks subpath to exports map

## Deliverables

1. Spike: verify @mswjs/source handles OpenAPI 3.1 (docs only mention 3.0 explicitly) — 5-min check against current openapi.json
2. fromOpenApi integration + stateful resource layer (@mswjs/data models for server/channel/user/invite)
3. Hand-rolled fromAsyncApi with pluggable msgpack encode/decode and link.trigger(opName, payload) helper for tests to push server-originated events
4. Subpath export + optional peerDeps in types-js package.json
5. CI drift check: every OpenAPI path x method has a handler; every AsyncAPI channel has a ws link (~20 LOC vitest)
6. README / docs for consumer usage patterns (libvex unit tests, desktop dev loop, mobile dev loop)
7. Wire into libvex's own test suite as a smoke test

## Open decisions

- OpenAPI 3.1 compat of @mswjs/source — docs only mention 3.0 explicitly. Spike required before committing.
- AsyncAPI router strategy — match frames by payload discriminator (Vex likely has type/op field). Expose user override for custom routing.
- Stateful scope for v1 — model server/channel/invite in @mswjs/data; skip message history and presence until real consumers ask for it.

## Related

- Complements vex-at4.15 (Microcks libvex CI), vex-at4.16 (Microcks spire CI)
- Depends on openapi.json + asyncapi.json being published from types-js (DONE — vex-spectral also passing)
title: Generate stateful MSW mocks from OpenAPI + AsyncAPI

description: Replace checkAuth middleware with Bearer-only (Authorization header). Remove cookie-parser dependency. Remove all res.cookie() calls from login, register, goodbye, and device-key auth endpoints. Login response returns { user, token } in body only — no Set-Cookie header.
title: [ADR-008] Spire: Bearer-only auth, remove cookies

description: The Syquel/mise-android-sdk plugin (and its canonical mise-plugins/mise-android-sdk cousin) both have a broken yq filter that doesn't handle the namespaced sdk:sdk-repository root in Google's repository2-3.xml. Both forks are unaware of the bug. Instead of patching upstream, switch to brew cask android-commandlinetools — fewer moving parts, no Lua/bash plugin maintenance, sdkmanager handles platforms/build-tools/system-images natively. Remove yq preflight, remove android-sdk tool from mise.toml, drop [hooks] + [tasks.'android:setup'], call mise-android-postinstall.sh directly from bootstrap.sh.
title: Migrate Android SDK bootstrap from mise plugin to brew cask android-commandlinetools

close_reason: Socket.dev CI steps added to all 5 repos
description: Add Socket CLI (@socketsecurity/cli) to CI workflows across all repos (types-js, crypto-js, libvex-js, spire, vex-chat monorepo). Socket scans for supply chain attacks, typosquatting, install scripts, and known vulnerabilities in dependencies. Add socket:scan npm script and GitHub Action step to PR checks.
title: Integrate Socket.dev supply chain security in all repos CI/CD

description: New method that performs the two-step HTTP handshake: POST /auth/device → sign challenge with device private key → POST /auth/device/verify → receive JWT. Sets this.token and cookie. Export from main entrypoint. Used by auto-login when stored credentials have a deviceKey.
title: [libvex] Client.loginWithDeviceKey() method

description: In bootstrap.ts autoLogin(), call client.loginWithDeviceKey(creds.deviceID) before connect(). Falls back to login screen if device key is revoked or challenge fails. No password needed, no JWT persisted — device key re-authenticates each app launch.
title: [store] autoLogin uses loginWithDeviceKey

description: Compare openapi.json against last published npm version. Fail CI on undocumented breaking changes to the API surface.
title: oasdiff breaking change detection in CI

description:
Replace swagger-ui-express with @scalar/express-api-reference for OpenAPI docs at /docs.
Add AsyncAPI viewer at /async-docs using @asyncapi/web-component (custom HTML element, no React/build step).
Serve asyncapi.json at /asyncapi.json (already done).

Endpoints:
- GET /docs — Scalar OpenAPI viewer (replaces Swagger UI)
- GET /async-docs — AsyncAPI web component viewer
- GET /openapi.json — raw OpenAPI spec (already done)
- GET /asyncapi.json — raw AsyncAPI spec (already done)

Implementation: static HTML file at public/async-docs.html with <asyncapi-component schemaUrl="/asyncapi.json">. No CDN — self-host @asyncapi/web-component from node_modules.
title: Replace Swagger UI with Scalar + add AsyncAPI web component viewer

close_reason: Implemented in commit 353adb5. @stoplight/spectral-cli@^6.15.0 installed, .spectral.yaml extends spectral:oas + spectral:asyncapi, npm run lint:specs script added, CI step in build.yml, both specs lint clean (0 errors/warnings).
description: Add @stoplight/spectral-cli to types-js CI. Lint openapi.json and asyncapi.json with spectral:oas and spectral:asyncapi rulesets. Create .spectral.yaml config.
title: Spectral lint for OpenAPI + AsyncAPI specs in CI
